req.title: System-Admin views org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-052 requires a system-admin to view an org-announcement via UI with mandatory field validation and audit record creation. Test coverage requires: (1) EP positive – valid admin views existing announcement, confirms 200 and audit record; (2) NEG negative – unauthenticated user is denied with 401; (3) NEG negative – non-admin role receives 403; (4) BVA boundary – announcement with title at maximum allowed character boundary; (5) STC state transition – module disabled then enabled, verifying access state change. This covers all error pathways and primary success path per R-WEB-052.

tests.name: test_admin_views_org_announcement_success
preconditions: At least one org-announcement record exists
expected: Announcement detail fields visible on screen
Audit record written for view action
trace: R-WEB-052: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_views_org_announcement_success(page, admin_session, seed_announcement, audit_client):
    announcement_id = seed_announcement["id"]
    title = seed_announcement["title"]

    admin_session(page)
    response = page.goto("/org-announcements")
    assert response.status == 200

    page.get_by_text(title).first.click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-detail']")).to_be_visible()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-title']")).to_have_text(title)

    audit_records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=announcement_id, action="view")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Expected at least one audit record for view action"
    assert audit_records[-1]["entity_id"] == str(announcement_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_org_announcement
preconditions: No authenticated session exists (browser storage cleared)
At least one org-announcement record exists
actions: Assert redirect to login page or 401 response
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_org_announcement(page, audit_client, seed_announcement):
    page.context.clear_cookies()
    response = page.goto("/org-announcements")

    is_redirected_to_login = "/login" in page.url
    is_401 = response is not None and response.status == 401
    assert is_redirected_to_login or is_401, (
        f"Expected redirect to /login or 401, got url={page.url} status={getattr(response,'status',None)}"
    )

    announcement_id = seed_announcement["id"]
    records_before = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=announcement_id, action="view")
    # no new audit record should appear
    assert len(records_before) == 0 or records_before[-1]["action"] != "view"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_viewing_org_announcement
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not admin)
At least one org-announcement record exists
actions: Assert 403 response or access-denied UI element
expected: HTTP 403 response or access-denied message rendered
No audit record written for this view attempt
trace: R-WEB-052: 403 if role forbidden error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_viewing_org_announcement(page, employee_session, seed_announcement, audit_client):
    announcement_id = seed_announcement["id"]
    employee_session(page)

    response = page.goto("/org-announcements")
    is_403 = response is not None and response.status == 403
    has_denied_ui = page.locator("[data-testid='access-denied']").is_visible()
    assert is_403 or has_denied_ui, (
        f"Expected 403 or access-denied UI, got status={getattr(response,'status',None)}"
    )

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-detail']")).not_to_be_visible()

    records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=announcement_id, action="view")
    assert all(r.get("actor_role") != "employee" for r in records), (
        "Unexpected audit record written for forbidden role"
    )

tests.name: test_admin_views_announcement_with_max_length_title
preconditions: An org-announcement exists with title exactly at MAX_TITLE_LENGTH characters
actions: Seed announcement with title of MAX_TITLE_LENGTH characters via <seed_max_title_announcement>
Select the max-title announcement and view detail
expected: Full title rendered without truncation error
trace: R-WEB-052: boundary on title length field (BVA)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TITLE_LENGTH = 255

def test_admin_views_announcement_with_max_length_title(page, admin_session, audit_client, api_client):
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LENGTH
    announcement = api_client.create_announcement({"title": max_title, "body": "Boundary test body"})
    announcement_id = announcement["id"]

    try:
        admin_session(page)
        response = page.goto("/org-announcements")
        assert response.status == 200

        page.get_by_text(max_title[:50]).first.click()
        detail = page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-title']")
        expect(detail).to_be_visible()
        assert len(detail.inner_text()) == MAX_TITLE_LENGTH

        records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=announcement_id, action="view")
        assert len(records) >= 1
    finally:
        api_client.delete_announcement(announcement_id)

tests.name: test_admin_views_announcement_after_module_re_enabled
preconditions: org-announcement module was previously disabled and has just been re-enabled
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements and view an announcement
expected: While disabled: module-disabled state prevents access
After re-enable: HTTP 200 and announcement detail visible
Audit record written after successful re-enabled view
trace: R-WEB-052: org-announcement module is enabled precondition
R-WEB-052: state transition from disabled to enabled
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_views_announcement_after_module_re_enabled(page, admin_session, seed_announcement, module_client, audit_client):
    announcement_id = seed_announcement["id"]
    title = seed_announcement["title"]

    module_client.disable("org-announcement")
    try:
        admin_session(page)
        response_disabled = page.goto("/org-announcements")
        disabled_status = getattr(response_disabled, "status", None)
        assert disabled_status in (404, 403) or page.locator("[data-testid='module-disabled']").is_visible()
    finally:
        module_client.enable("org-announcement")

    response_enabled = page.goto("/org-announcements")
    assert response_enabled.status == 200

    page.get_by_text(title).first.click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-detail']")).to_be_visible()

    records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=announcement_id, action="view")
    assert len(records) >= 1, "Audit record expected after module re-enabled view"
